title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The history of cinema (0) back to the late 19th century when inventors first began experimenting with moving images. The Lumière brothers, who are widely (1) as the pioneers of cinema, held their first public screening in Paris in 1895. Early films were silent and usually (2) only a few minutes. However, audiences were (3) by this new form of entertainment. The introduction of sound in the 1920s (4) about a dramatic transformation in the industry. Many silent film stars struggled to (5) to the new technology, while others thrived. Hollywood soon (6) as the centre of the film world, attracting talented actors and directors from around the globe. The studio system that developed there (7) rise to the golden age of cinema. Today, the film industry continues to evolve with digital technology, but the magic of cinema that (8) audiences over a century ago remains unchanged.

example.explanation: 'Dates back to' is the fixed expression to indicate the temporal origin of something.

questions.explanation: 'Regarded as' is the correct collocation to express how someone is regarded.
'Lasted' is the correct verb to indicate the duration of something.
'Gave rise to' (with 'rise' already in the text) means to originate or cause something.
'Brought about' (with 'about' already in the text) means to cause or bring about a change.
'Adapt to' is the correct expression to adjust to new circumstances.
'Emerged as' indicates that something gradually became important.
'Gave rise to' (with 'rise' already in the text) means to originate or cause something.
'Captivated' expresses completely fascinating or captivating someone.
